scenario_results.reason: Accepted complete affordable-unit intake for downstream review with property, unit, applicant, household, income, waitlist, authorization, and source-system evidence while preserving approval boundaries.
Pended intake when core property, jurisdiction, program, unit, applicant, policy, authorization, and system prerequisites were missing.
Rejected or pended incomplete application due to household mismatch, unsigned consent, expired income evidence, missing student status, and absent waitlist timestamp.
Routed final denial, rent exception, lease signing, and policy waiver requests to the proper approval owners without making the decisions.
Refused screening progression from stale authorization and missing FCRA adverse-action handling context.
Held voucher lease-up intake until RFTA, inspection, rent reasonableness, tenancy addendum, lease, and HAP owner context are present.
Recognized LIHTC income-limit, student-rule, household, rent-limit, and tenant-income-certification evidence as required industry-specific prerequisites.
Routed legal defensibility and accommodation merits questions to fair housing, reasonable accommodation, and legal owners.
Pended public housing admission intake when admission policy, waitlist selection, occupancy standard, eligible immigration, income verification, and EIV retrieval were missing.
Blocked move-in setup where inspection, accessibility, move-in, key, and access authorization facts were unresolved.
Escalated conflicting waitlist skip, tenant selection, program guidance, and property system records without unsupported synthesis.
Adapted the checklist to stricter tenant local rules while preserving public baseline assumptions, retrieval dependencies, and authority boundaries.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, intake-access-and-initiation, and cross-industry abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 property intake and leasing scenarios.

Runtime remains specification-based and no final leasing approval, denial, legal interpretation, reasonable accommodation decision, voucher approval, LIHTC certification, rent setting, lease signing, or licensed brokerage authority is claimed.
